title: Food System Nutrient Independence
subtitle: Breaking the Fossil Fuel Grip on the Global Food Supply
core_concept: Industrial food production extracts nutrients from finite geological deposits and fossil fuels, applies them inefficiently (17-20% uptake), and allows the remainder to cascade as pollution. Breaking this loop requires closing nutrient cycles, decarbonizing nitrogen fixation, and redesigning farming architectures.
through_line: The spaces between our dependencies are where sovereignty lives. Giving people their lives back starts at the soil level.

1.name: The Nitrogen Crisis
1.content: Haber-Bosch process: 150-230 MMT ammonia/yr, 3-5% world natural gas, 1-2% global CO2, 50% food dependency. Nitrogen cascade: 17-20% uptake, 400+ dead zones, N2O (300x CO2 GWP). FAO: 1.78B people dependent on imported N or gas.

2.name: The Phosphorus Predicament
2.content: Phosphate rock: 85% in 5 countries, Morocco 73-85%, US ~25 yr domestic. No atmospheric analog. 2008 price spike 800%. Peak P models 2033-2070+. Quality declining, costs rising. 90% mined P used in agriculture.

3.name: Biological Nitrogen Fixation Alternatives
3.content: 6 BNF pathways: symbiotic (Rhizobium), associative (Azospirillum), free-living (Azotobacter), cyanobacterial (Anabaena/Azolla), PGPR consortia, engineered microbes (Pivot Bio). 20-50% synthetic N replacement. Commercial status and field variability.

4.name: Green Ammonia and Electrochemical Pathways
4.content: Green ammonia (electrolysis + H-B, TRL 7-8), eNRR (TRL 3-4), Li-mediated NRR (TRL 4-5), plasma-catalytic (TRL 4-5, Nitricity/NitroCapt), photocatalytic (TRL 2-3). Key players: Yara, CF Industries, Jupiter Ionics, Nitrofix, Nium. WEF Top 10 Emerging Tech 2025.

5.name: Phosphorus Recovery and Circular Systems
5.content: Struvite (MgNH4PO4·6H2O): 10% Mg, 7% N, 39% P, slow-release 44-63 days. Recovery: wastewater 10-60%, sewage sludge 35-70%, SSA up to 84.92%, animal manure 20-50%. Global wastewater P: 3.7 MT/yr = 20% ag demand. Ostara PEARL, PhosphoGREEN, ANPHOS. EU Regulation 2021/1165. Legacy P: WSU 100-year supply in over-applied soils.

6.name: Alternative Farming Architectures
6.content: Hydroponics: 90-95% water savings, 20x lettuce yield. Vertical farming: 38.8 kWh/kg vs 5.4 greenhouse, 28-40% LED energy savings 2025. Aeroponics: 90% less water than hydroponics. Aquaponics: closed fish-plant loop. Regen ag: 5 core principles, 50 lb/ac/yr N fixation via grass-legume, legacy P mobilization, soil microbiome. Crop type limitation for CEA.

milestones.milestone: Scale biofertilizers to 10% of N demand
Deploy struvite recovery at 500+ wastewater plants
BNF covers 30-50% of N in major cereal crops
Struvite covers 15-20% of global P demand
Closed-loop P system for 50% of agriculture
Fossil-free nitrogen fixation for 50%+ of crops

safety_critical.rule: All citations traceable to peer-reviewed, government, or professional org sources. Zero entertainment media.
Every percentage, metric ton figure, TRL rating, cost estimate attributed to specific named source.
Present evidence without advocating for specific legislation or policy positions.
Western Sahara/Morocco phosphate noted as disputed; no political position taken.
N2O GWP values, CO2 estimates attributed to IPCC or peer-reviewed sources.
No human health claims beyond peer-reviewed evidence.
Technology Readiness Level assessments match published assessments; no overclaiming.
No findings taken out of context; limitations documented alongside benefits.

success_criteria.description: Haber-Bosch fossil fuel dependency fully quantified: energy (3-5% gas), CO2 (1-2%), food dependency (50%)
Phosphorus geopolitical concentration mapped: reserves by country, price history, Morocco dominance (73-85%)
All six biological nitrogen fixation pathways documented with mechanism, yield data, commercial readiness
Green ammonia pathways compared with TRL: electrolysis-based, eNRR, Li-mediated, plasma, photocatalytic
P recovery potential quantified from 4+ source streams with recovery rates and technology names
Struvite properties documented: composition, slow-release (44-63 days), EU certification, heavy metal concerns
Hydroponics/vertical farming data: water savings (90-95%), yield multipliers (20x), energy (38.8 vs 5.4 kWh/kg)
Regenerative ag nutrient mechanisms: legacy P, grass-legume N fixation (50 lb/ac/yr), soil microbiome role
All alternatives rated on 4 dimensions: scalability, technology readiness, fossil fuel reduction, timeline
Transition roadmap: 3 near-term (2025-2030), 3 medium-term (2030-2040), 2 long-term (2040-2050) milestones
All numerical claims attributed to specific peer-reviewed, government, or professional org sources
Source bibliography: 5+ peer-reviewed, 3+ government/agency (FAO, USGS, EPA), 2+ professional org sources
